steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a "most logically completes the text" question. The decisive clue is that Gidra had an "expansive reach" despite only printing 4,000 copies per month — so the correct choice must explain how influence outpaced circulation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The students who initially formed Gidra each contributed financially to its creation.': Funding sources say nothing about how the newsletter's reach exceeded its print run. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In addition to covering current events, Gidra also featured works of art and literature.': Content variety does not explain how 4,000 copies could influence more than 4,000 people. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Gidra was initially based out of the Asian American Studies Center at UCLA.': Physical location tells us nothing about circulation or extended readership. ✗
  • Choice D — 'People would often give their copies of Gidra to others once they had finished reading an issue.': If each copy passed through multiple hands, actual readers would far exceed 4,000 — directly explaining the "expansive reach." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
